item.title: Norway crown princess's son sentenced to 4 years in prison for rape
item.description: Marius Borg Hoiby, the eldest son of Norway's Crown Princess Mette-Marit, has been sentenced to four years in prison.

item.title: Tehran and Israel voice caveats on U.S.-Iran deal set for Friday signing
item.description: Israel says its forces will remain in Lebanon and Iran says it will get half of its frozen funds before final talks with the U.S. begin.

item.title: 3,600 stock trades in 3 months: Breaking down Trump's flurry of investments
item.description: President Trump's investment accounts traded between $212 million and $695 million in stocks and other securities in the first three months of the year — an unprecedented sum for a sitting president.

item.title: Explore Trump's 3,600 stock trades from the first 3 months of 2026
item.description: View the companies and sectors the president's investment accounts bought and sold.

item.title: Transcript: Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth on "Face the Nation with Margaret Brennan," June 14, 2026
item.description: The following is the transcript of the interview with Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth that aired on "Face the Nation with Margaret Brennan" on June 14, 2026.

item.title: White House hosts "UFC Freedom 250" fight on Trump's 80th birthday
item.description: The UFC hosted a fight series on the White House South Lawn Sunday night.

item.title: Referee denied entry into U.S. was talking to "very bad people," official says
item.description: Andrew Giuliani, the executive director of the White House's World Cup task force and the son of former NYC Mayor Rudy Giuliani, said he stood by the U.S.'s decision to reject Omar Artan.

item.title: Full Interview: Trump World Cup chief Andrew Giuliani on referee's deportation, Iran's team, more
item.description: Andrew Giuliani, the executive director of the White House Task Force on the World Cup, speaks with CBS News' Camilo Montoya-Galvez about the deportation of Somali referee Omar Artan, the conditions under which the Iranian team can play in the U.S. and more.

item.title: World Cup kicks off with 3 opening ceremonies, 48 teams, billions of fans
item.description: With matches being played in 11 cities across the U.S., Mexico and Canada​, fans are getting three World Cup opening ceremonies.

item.title: Deadly Russian strikes leave landmark Ukraine cathedral in flames
item.description: Russia fired a barrage of missiles at several major Ukrainian cities, killing at least 11 people and sparking a blaze at one of the most important Orthodox monasteries.

item.title: All Iranian World Cup players will be allowed in the U.S., White House official says
item.description: Andrew Giuliani, the executive director of the White House Task Force on the World Cup, says all the players from Iran's World Cup team will be allowed in the U.S. for their matches, but acknowledges some members of the team's support staff have been denied U.S. entry.

item.title: "A few" World Cup players were referred for secondary questioning upon U.S. entry, Giuliani says
item.description: While no World Cup players have been blocked from entering the U.S., "a few" have been referred for secondary questioning, according to Andrew Giuliani, the executive director of the White House Task Force on the World Cup.

item.title: Man caught with 150 live venomous scorpions at airport
item.description: Police released an image of the venomous scorpions, which appear to be individually wrapped in plastic.

item.title: Somali World Cup ref denied U.S. entry was talking to "very bad people," White House official says
item.description: Andrew Giuliani, the executive director of the White House Task Force on the World Cup, is defending the decision to bar U.S. entry to Somali World Cup referee Omar Artan, saying he was talking to "very bad people."

item.title: Woman dies after being thrown from bridge without safety cord
item.description: In videos of the accident circulating online, two men launch the woman off Skeleton Bridge, while onlookers realize there is no safety mechanism attached.

item.title: They're uninsured after Obamacare became too costly. They're far from alone.
item.description: After congressional Republicans let expanded subsidies for Affordable Care Act plans expire at the end of last year, some families have decided the price is too great of a financial burden and canceled their coverage.

item.title: Iran and U.S. reach peace deal, Trump and Pakistani prime minister say
item.description: Pakistan's prime minister said Sunday the U.S. and Iran had reached a deal that includes "the immediate and permanent termination of military operations on all fronts, including in Lebanon."

item.title: Trump says U.S. deal with Iran "is now complete"
item.description: The U.S. and Iran are expected to meet for a signing ceremony on Friday, June 19, in Switzerland, Pakistan's prime minister said.

item.title: The 2026 FIFA World Cup schedule and how to watch
item.description: With 104 World Cup games being played in the U.S., Canada and Mexico, it's like "a Super Bowl every single day for five weeks," U.S. team captain Tim Ream told CBS News.

item.title: Carolina Hurricanes win second Stanley Cup, beating Vegas in 6 games
item.description: The Golden Knights had taken a two-games-to-one lead in the Stanley Cup Finals, but Carolina came roaring back, winning three straight to take home their first championship since the 2005-2006 season.

item.title: 6/14: CBS Weekend News
item.description: Trump says U.S.-Iran deal "is now complete," U.S. ending blockade on Strait of Hormuz; Inside Trump's trip to the G7 Summit.

item.title: 6/14/2026: Here Come the Humanoids; The Empty Rooms; Lamine Yamal
item.description: First, a look at the progress made on AI-powered humanoid robots. Then, seeing the rooms left behind after school shootings. And, Lamine Yamal: The 60 Minutes Interview.

item.title: Inside Trump's trip to the G7 Summit
item.description: President Trump will depart the nation's capital Sunday night for the G7 Summit in France. Nancy Cordes reports on what's ahead.

item.title: What to know about U.S.-Iran deal announced by Trump
item.description: Olivia Gazis and Imtiaz Tyab report on the U.S.-Iran deal announced by President Trump on Sunday.

item.title: Georgia lawmakers to return for special session on redistricting, election system
item.description: Gov. Brian Kemp has called lawmakers back to Atlanta to redraw congressional districts for 2028 and address Georgia's ballot-counting system before summer elections.

item.title: Mitch McConnell admitted to the hospital, spokesperson says
item.description: Sen. Mitch McConnell was admitted to the hospital Sunday morning, a spokesperson for the Republican confirmed to CBS News.

item.title: Organic formula recalled after 3 babies diagnosed with infant botulism
item.description: The affected formula was sold at Target and at Nara.com, according to the Food and Drug Administration.

item.title: Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth, Sen. Warner, more | Face the Nation Full Broadcast - June 14, 2026
item.description: This week on "Face the Nation with Margaret Brennan," as Qatari mediators travel to Tehran to finalize the truce in the U.S.-Iran war, Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth joins. Plus, Democratic Sens. Mark Warner and Mark Kelly join.

item.title: Early results indicate defeat of measure that would cap Swiss population
item.description: The number of people living in Switzerland has soared by nearly one-quarter over the last generation.

item.title: Vance: Trump "very supportive" as vice president moves closer to 2028 decision
item.description: Vice President JD Vance tells "CBS Sunday Morning" that he and his wife, Usha, will make a decision whether to enter the 2028 presidential race following the 2026 midterm elections.
